info.name: Granola API
info.description: Every endpoint the @alavida-ai/granola-core SDK uses. Set the `GRANOLA_API_KEY` collection variable to your personal key (`grn_…`) before sending requests. All endpoints are read-only — Granola's public API exposes no write endpoints.

variable.description: Personal API key (`grn_…`). Generate one in the Granola desktop app: Settings → API → Create new key.
Replace with a real note id — get one from `Notes > List notes (basic)`. Pattern: `not_<14 chars>`.
Opaque pagination cursor from a prior response's `cursor` field.
ISO-8601 lower bound for date-filter examples.
ISO-8601 upper bound for date-filter examples.

item.name: 1. Notes
item.description: List and fetch meeting notes. Read-only.

request.description: 10 most recent notes, newest first. Returns `{notes: [...], hasMore: bool, cursor: string|null}`. Each note is a NoteSummary (id, title, owner, created_at, updated_at). Used by SDK's `client.notes.list({ pageSize: 10 })`.
Notes created within a date range. Useful for daily/weekly digests. Used by SDK's `client.notes.list({ pageSize, createdAfter, createdBefore })`.

item.name: GET /v1/notes — list notes (incremental sync via updated_after)

request.description: Notes whose `updated_at` is on/after a watermark — the standard shape for an incremental sync job. Pair with a stored watermark from the last successful run. Used by SDK's `client.notes.list({ updatedAfter })`.

query.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response. Set the CURSOR collection variable first.

request.description: Fetch the next page. Set the `CURSOR` collection variable to the `cursor` field from a previous response. Used by SDK's `client.notes.list({ cursor })` (or `client.notes.iterate({...})` under the hood).

One note: id, title, owner, web_url, created_at/updated_at, attendees, calendar_event, folder_membership, summary_text, summary_markdown. Transcript is omitted. Used by SDK's `client.notes.get({ noteId })`.

query.description: Opt in to the transcript array. Can be large; omit when you only need the summary.

request.description: Same as above plus a `transcript: [{speaker, text, start_time, end_time}, ...]` array. Speaker labels depend on recording source — desktop = `microphone` / `speaker` channels (Me/Them), iPhone face-to-face = real per-person diarization. Used by SDK's `client.notes.get({ noteId, includeTranscript: true })`.

item.name: 2. Folders
item.description: Folder catalog. Folders are tag-like, not directories — see `folder_membership` on `GET /v1/notes/{id}` for the note-to-folder mapping.

request.description: All folders (paginated). Each folder has `id`, `name`, `parent_folder_id` (null for top-level), `created_at`, `updated_at`. Build the tree client-side. Used by SDK's `client.folders.list({ pageSize: 30 })`.

query.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response.

request.description: Next page of folders. Used by SDK's `client.folders.list({ cursor })`.

item.name: 3. Identity (no /me endpoint)
item.description: Granola exposes no /me. Identity is inferred from the `owner.email` field across recent notes — the most frequent owner is the likely caller. Personal keys also see notes shared by collaborators, so the result is a probabilistic guess.

request.description: Sample 30 recent notes. Tally `owner.email` client-side; the most frequent owner is 'likely you'. Implemented in SDK as `inferIdentity(client, sample = 30)` (returns `{ sampleSize, likelyYou, allOwners }`). The CLI's `granola whoami` is a thin wrapper around this.
